title: Pull-Request and Code Review Evidence for Payment Microservices

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 6.3.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 6.3.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.36: Pull-Request and Code Review Evidence for Payment Microservices.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.36' running on index=devsecops sourcetype="github:pull_request" — repo, merged, review_count, labels, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Custom HEC `sourcetype="github:pull_request"` or Splunk Add-on for Bitbucket if used
spl:
index=devsecops sourcetype="github:pull_request" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_payment_repos.csv repo OUTPUT pci_repo
| where pci_repo="true" AND merged="true"
| eval review_ok=if(review_count>=1 AND match(labels,"(?i)security-reviewed"),1,0)
| where review_ok=0
| table _time, repo, user, review_count, labels, url
| sort _time
description: Ingests Git platform webhooks to verify merges to payment repositories include required reviewers, supporting secure software engineering practices for custom payment software.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes devsecops and sourcetypes github:pull_request. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Configure org-wide webhooks with HEC token. (2) Align label policy with secure SDLC. (3) Block deploy pipeline on Splunk summary alert if desired. (4) Monthly export for PCI evidence binder. Indexes required: `index=devsecops`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=github:pull_request`; Lookups: `pci_payment_repos`, `pci_payment_repos.csv`.
visualization: Table (exceptions), pie (review_ok ratio overall), timeline (merge times).
knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom HEC `sourcetype="github:pull_request"` or Splunk Add-on for Bitbucket if used.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=devsecops` `sourcetype="github:pull_request"` — `repo`, `merged`, `review_count`, `labels`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Configure org-wide webhooks with HEC token. (2) Align label policy with secure SDLC. (3) Block deploy pipeline on Splunk summary alert if desired. (4) Monthly export for PCI evidence binder.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devsecops sourcetype="github:pull_request" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_payment_repos.csv repo OUTPUT pci_repo
| where pci_repo="true" AND merged="true"
| eval review_ok=if(review_count>=1 AND match(labels,"(?i)security-reviewed"),1,0)
| where review_ok=0
| table _time, repo, user, review_count, labels, url
| sort _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Pull-Request and Code Review Evidence for Payment Microservices (PCI DSS Req 6.2.4, 6.3.1)** — Ingests Git platform webhooks to verify merges to payment repositories include required reviewers, supporting secure software engineering practices for custom payment software.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=devsecops` `sourcetype="github:pull_request"` — `repo`, `merged`, `review_count`, `labels`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom HEC `sourcetype="github:pull_request"` or Splunk Add-on for Bitbucket if used. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devsecops; **sourcetype**: github:pull_request. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=devsecops, sourcetype="github:pull_request", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where pci_repo="true" AND merged="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **review_ok** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where review_ok=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: _time, labels, repo, review_count, review_ok, url, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (exceptions), pie (review_ok ratio overall), timeline (merge times).
grandmaExplanation: We ingests Git platform webhooks to verify merges to payment repositories include required reviewers, supporting secure software engineering practices for custom payment software. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Change (CIM data model), All_Changes dataset.
